task_completion.description: Verify that OpenBCI GUI is running and the data source is set to Synthetic mode.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the OpenBCI GUI application window. In the system control panel or data source selection area (usually at the top or startup screen), 'Synthetic' should be selected or displayed as the active session type.

task_completion.description: Verify the workspace is configured with a 4-panel layout containing the specific required widgets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the main GUI interface. It must be divided into exactly 4 panels. Panel 1 (typically top-left) must display 'Time Series', Panel 2 (typically top-right) must display 'FFT Plot', Panel 3 (typically bottom-left) must display 'Band Power', and Panel 4 (typically bottom-right) must display 'Focus'.

task_completion.description: Verify the bandpass filter is set to 1-40 Hz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the filter settings, often located within the Time Series widget controls or global hardware settings. The bandpass filter (BP Filt) should explicitly show '1-40 Hz' or low cutoff of 1.0 and high cutoff of 40.0.

task_completion.description: Verify that Expert Mode is enabled in the GUI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an 'Expert Mode' toggle or button in the GUI (often in the top right dropdown menu or main settings). It should be visually indicated as active, toggled on, or checked.

task_completion.description: Verify a screenshot of the workspace was saved to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file manager window, terminal output, or an in-app GUI notification confirming that an image file was successfully saved to the '~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the configuration settings file was exported to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file manager window, terminal output, or an in-app GUI notification confirming that a settings/configuration file was successfully saved to the '~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Settings/' directory.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually used the OpenBCI GUI to configure the workspace.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the OpenBCI GUI open with evidence of interaction (e.g., dropdown menus open, cursor clicking on widget selection, or settings panels visible), rather than just terminal commands.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not bypass the GUI export/save features by manually creating mock files in the terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshot and settings files should be generated via the OpenBCI GUI's built-in save/export buttons or keyboard shortcuts. Look for the absence of commands like 'touch ~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Settings/config.json' or 'echo > ...' in any visible terminal windows.

integrity.description: Ensure the session is actually active and streaming synthetic data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Time Series and FFT Plot widgets should display active, populated waveforms and data graphs, not empty grids or static 'Start Data Stream' placeholder screens.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2. The data source must be set to SYNTHETIC. The layout must be exactly 4 panels containing: Time Series, FFT Plot, Band Power, and Focus widgets. The bandpass filter must be set to 1.0 - 40.0 Hz. Expert Mode must be enabled (which is required to capture screenshots via shortcut keys). The VLM should verify that a screenshot was saved to ~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/ and a settings file was saved to ~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Settings/.
